title: SQL Support on Sybase
text: SQL Support on Sybase The Sybase connector fully supports the sql ProcScript command and SQL Workbench. All commands which can be executed using the Sybase ISQL utility can be executed using the sql instruction or in the SQL Workbench. Multiple SQL statements are allowed within one ProcScript statement, or can be executed together in the Workbench. However, transaction management should be avoided because this can confuse the SYB connector’s transaction manager. ProcScript Statement sql In addition to the standard SQL statements, the sql instruction can also use Sybase stored procedures, as shown below: sql "exec tp1 ’%%bank’,’%%branch’,’%%teller’,’%%amount’", "syb" Copy The double percent signs ( %% ) substitute a value from the form and pass it to the stored procedure. Single Quote Character Strings The connector uses the Sybase quoted identifier feature, so all references to data which is a character string must use single quotes ( ’ ) instead of double quotes ( " ). For example: select * from table1 where name = ’Tommy’ Copy Numeric and Identity Fields A bug in the Sybase .0.1 client may affects the processing of Numeric and Identity fields in the SQL Workbench and sql ProcScript statements. All select statements which access Numeric or Identity data must be modified. Instead of: select numeric_field, identity_field from table Copy use the following SQL instruction: select convert(char, numeric_field), convert(char, identity_field) from table Copy Display Format for Datetime Data Sybase has a default Date format which has a specific layout and a minimal amount of precision, as follows: Jan 28 1994 12:11 PM Copy The sql ProcScript command and the SQL Workbench use the Sybase Client-Library routines for conversion, which give additional precision to dates that are displayed. To display a date with default precision, modify the SQL instruction. Instead of: select date_field() from table1 Copy use the following SQL instruction: select convert(char, date_field) from table1 Copy Display Format for Money Data Sybase has a default Money format which has four digits following the decimal, and no commas: 12.1234 Copy The sql ProcScript command and the SQL Workbench use the Sybase Client-Library routines for conversion which do not display money with the default format. To display money with the correct format, make one of the following modifications to the SQL instruction. Instead of: select money_field from table1 Copy use the following SQL instruction to retrieve a character string: select convert(char, convert(numeric(20,4), money_field)) from table1 Copy or use the following SQL instruction to retrieve a Floating Point value: select convert(numeric(20,4), money_field) from table1 Copy Monitoring SQL Using the Message Frame If the /pri switch is set to a value greater than or equal to 32, the SQL generated by the SYB connector is sent to the message frame. Any information from Sybase SQL Server options such as showplan also appears in the message frame when the SQL Workbench is used. All error messages from Sybase are always sent to the message frame. In addition, user-defined messages from stored procedures which use the raiserror statement also appear in the message frame. Related Links sql Pass a DML statement to the specified DBMS path. SQL Editor Use the SQL Editor to send ad hoc queries to the database. This is only supported by databases that include a Data Manipulation Language (DML). /pri Specify the types of messages sent to the message frame or log file. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
